course_description.overview: Urban start from Panama City featuring views of the Panama Canal and Bridge of the Americas, with 1,680m total elevation gain over approximately 125 km. Central America's largest cycling gran fondo offering three distance options.

key_segments.name: Panama Canal View
key_segments.description: Iconic urban section with views of the Panama Canal.

key_segments.name: Bridge of the Americas
key_segments.description: Passage offering views of the famous bridge.

key_segments.name: Climbing Sections
key_segments.description: Moderate terrain with rolling short steep hills or sustained climbs totaling 1,680m elevation.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Moderate difficulty with rolling terrain, short steep hills, and sustained climbs suitable for experienced amateur cyclists.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Tocumen International Airport (PTY) near Panama City. Event starts in Panama City.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and accommodations available in Panama City; book early for event weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Registration typically opens months in advance; check official site for 2026 details. Similar events open in December.
logistics.bike_logistics: Fly with bike to PTY; local bike shops in Panama City for assembly and service.
logistics.local_considerations: Tropical climate: hot and humid; hydrate well. Traffic and road conditions in urban start; Spanish primary language.

final_verdict.one_liner: Central America's premier gran fondo with stunning canal views and challenging climbs.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking a scenic, moderately challenging event in an exotic location with iconic landmarks.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unique Panama Canal experience and status as regional flagship event.
pro_tips: Train for heat and humidity in tropical conditions.
Start hydration early due to urban heat.
Book flights and lodging early for Panama City.
Opt for the 125km for full canal and bridge views.
